task: Create a Users admin page with search + pagination and implement Next.js API endpoints to list users, update email/active status, and reset password.

steps.analysis: Create a simple deterministic user store with seed users. Expose functions for listUsers(query,page,pageSize), updateUser(id,{email,active}), and resetPassword(id) that returns a temporary password string. Keep it in a single module used by API route handlers.
Implement GET /api/users with pagination + search. Read query/page/pageSize from URL search params. Return JSON { items, total } with stable ordering.
Implement PATCH /api/users/:id to update email and/or active status. Validate inputs. Return updated user JSON. Use the shared store module.
Implement POST /api/users/:id/reset-password that resets and returns JSON { ok: true, tempPassword } (or { ok: true } if you prefer). Use the shared store module.

Create app/users/page.tsx (client component) that fetches /api/users with query/page/pageSize. Render a table with columns Nickname, Email, Active, Actions. Include search input, pagination controls, and per-row actions: toggle active (PATCH), edit email (inline edit or modal -> PATCH), reset password (POST) with a confirmation and show temp password if returned. Keep styling minimal and follow existing conventions.
